authority_boundary.allowed_claim: Public provider context recipes can describe bounded byte budgets, ordered section fill, omitted-section manifests, graph roles, and reducer deliverable types.
forbidden_claims: proof bodies or oracle-only truth-side material may enter public context
Lean/Lake or Mathlib-dependent proof execution is authorized
release, publication, or recipient work is authorized

validation_rules: Every recipe budget must match the public recipe table ceiling.
Recipes must fill sections in order and emit omitted-section manifests when material is excluded by the budget.
Truth-side, oracle-only, provider body, and proof body fields are forbidden.
Provider call authority must stay false.
Every recipe deliverable type must match its reducer route contract.
Release, provider, proof-authority, and Lean/Lake authority overclaims are rejected.
receipt_expectations: result receipt with recipe projections and omitted-section metadata
board receipt with public contract and authority ceiling
anti_claim: This standard governs public provider context recipe budgets only; it does not call providers, expose proof bodies, certify theorem correctness, authorize Lean/Lake, authorize release, or prove private macro equivalence.
